results.possibleIdentity: Dr. Zahoor M. Alvi (Ph.D.) was a radiation medical physicist and engineer primarily active in the Los Angeles area during the late 1960s and 1970s. He earned his Ph.D. in Engineering from the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) in December 1968. Professionally, he was a long-time staff member at the Kaiser Foundation Hospital (Kaiser Medical Center) in Los Angeles, where he specialized in radiation physics and the application of engineering to medicine. His technical work included developing mathematical models for human body electrolytes and researching radiation sensitivity. Dr. Alvi was an active member of the scientific community, delivering lectures at UCLA and participating as a consultant for the United Nations as early as 1961. He is associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, where he likely served as a technical advisor, scientific journalist, or editor, bridging the gap between engineering and the arts. His expertise covered diverse fields including bio-engineering, medicine, physics, cybernetics, and chemistry, and he previously served as an instructor of pharmaceutical chemistry at North Dakota State University. He was a resident of Beverly Hills and Los Angeles, California.
